title: tokio -- Async Runtime
description: The most popular async runtime for Rust. Tokio provides async I/O, timers, task spawning, synchronization primitives, and channels. It powers most async Rust applications including web servers, database clients, and network services.

commonPatterns.title: Running multiple tasks concurrently
Select the first completed future
Channels for message passing
Shared state with Mutex

whenToUse: When you need async I/O: web servers, API clients, database connections, file I/O at scale, network protocols, or any application that benefits from non-blocking concurrency.
alternatives: async-std (similar API to std), smol (lightweight), monoio (io_uring-based, thread-per-core). For simple concurrency without async: rayon (data parallelism), crossbeam (channels and scoped threads).
